member.name: M:PulseMonitor.Collections.BatchObservableCollection`1.PushBatch(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0},System.Int32)
member.summary: A performance-optimized ClearAndBatch that keeps the collection at a fixed size (circular-buffer like behaviour after reaching maxPoints).

member.name: T:PulseMonitor.Collections.BufferedObservableCollection`1
member.summary: A high-performance ObservableCollection that supports batch updates to minimize UI thread notifications and layout passes.

member.name: M:PulseMonitor.Collections.BufferedObservableCollection`1.AddRange(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Adds a range of items to the collection and fires only ONE notification.

member.name: M:PulseMonitor.Collections.BufferedObservableCollection`1.ReplaceWithSnapshot(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{`0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Replaces current values with a snapshot and sends only ONE reset notification.

member.name: M:PulseMonitor.Collections.BufferedObservableCollection`1.RemoveRange(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes a range of items and fires only ONE notification.

member.name: T:PulseMonitor.Hardware.AiDiagnosticResult
member.summary: Carries the HRV diagnostic result emitted by the ESP32-S3 firmware approximately every 5 seconds when enough RR-intervals are available.
member.param: Board millis() at the time of computation.
SDNN (ms) — standard deviation of NN intervals. ≥50 ms = healthy.
RMSSD (ms) — root mean square of successive differences.
Rhythm classification string: "Normal", "Tachycardia", "Bradycardia", "Irregular".
0=Low, 1=Moderate, 2=High, 3=VeryHigh (SDNN-based sympathovagal balance).

member.name: M:PulseMonitor.Hardware.AiDiagnosticResult.#ctor(System.Int64,System.Single,System.Single,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Carries the HRV diagnostic result emitted by the ESP32-S3 firmware approximately every 5 seconds when enough RR-intervals are available.
member.param: Board millis() at the time of computation.
SDNN (ms) — standard deviation of NN intervals. ≥50 ms = healthy.
RMSSD (ms) — root mean square of successive differences.
Rhythm classification string: "Normal", "Tachycardia", "Bradycardia", "Irregular".
0=Low, 1=Moderate, 2=High, 3=VeryHigh (SDNN-based sympathovagal balance).

member.name: P:PulseMonitor.Hardware.AiDiagnosticResult.Timestamp
member.summary: Board millis() at the time of computation.

member.name: P:PulseMonitor.Hardware.AiDiagnosticResult.Sdnn
member.summary: SDNN (ms) — standard deviation of NN intervals. ≥50 ms = healthy.

member.name: P:PulseMonitor.Hardware.AiDiagnosticResult.Rmssd
member.summary: RMSSD (ms) — root mean square of successive differences.

member.name: P:PulseMonitor.Hardware.AiDiagnosticResult.Rhythm
member.summary: Rhythm classification string: "Normal", "Tachycardia", "Bradycardia", "Irregular".

member.name: P:PulseMonitor.Hardware.AiDiagnosticResult.StressLevel
member.summary: 0=Low, 1=Moderate, 2=High, 3=VeryHigh (SDNN-based sympathovagal balance).

member.name: E:PulseMonitor.Hardware.ISampleDataReader.AiDiagnosticReceived
member.summary: Raised ~every 5 s when the firmware sends an HRV diagnostic packet.

member.name: T:PulseMonitor.Processing.FftProcessor
member.summary:
Simple 64-point DFT over RR-interval time series. Produces LF (0.04–0.15 Hz) and HF (0.15–0.40 Hz) power bands used in frequency-domain HRV analysis.
For 100 bpm max, RR ≈ 600 ms → effective sample rate ~1.67 Hz when interpolated. We interpolate the RR series to a uniform 4 Hz grid before DFT.

member.name: M:PulseMonitor.Processing.FftProcessor.Compute(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Int64})
member.summary: Computes the power spectrum of the given RR intervals (ms). Returns a with per-bin magnitude and LF/HF power.

member.name: T:PulseMonitor.Processing.FrequencyBin
member.summary: Single frequency bin (Hz, magnitude).

member.name: M:PulseMonitor.Processing.FrequencyBin.#ctor(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Single frequency bin (Hz, magnitude).

member.name: T:PulseMonitor.Processing.FrequencySpectrum
member.summary: Full power spectrum result from .

member.name: P:PulseMonitor.Processing.FrequencySpectrum.LfHfRatio
member.summary: LF/HF ratio — sympathovagal balance indicator.

member.name: T:PulseMonitor.Processing.HrvProcessor
member.summary: Client-side HRV processor — mirrors the on-device algorithm. Used in the MAUI app to supplement / verify firmware HRV output, and to enable offline HRV computation for sessions recorded before firmware upgrade.

member.name: M:PulseMonitor.Processing.HrvProcessor.OnPeak(System.Int64)
member.summary: Feed a new peak timestamp (ms). Call whenever a BPM peak is detected. Returns null until enough intervals are collected.

member.name: T:PulseMonitor.Processing.HrvMetrics
member.summary: HRV metrics for display in the AI Diagnostics tab.

member.name: M:PulseMonitor.Processing.HrvMetrics.#ctor(System.Single,System.Single,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: HRV metrics for display in the AI Diagnostics tab.

member.name: T:PulseMonitor.ViewModels.MainViewModel
member.summary: Atomic Performance Architecture: 1. WebSocket callback (100Hz) → enqueue to ConcurrentQueue (zero UI work) 2. DispatcherTimer (10Hz) → drain queue into circular ring buffers 3. One buffered collection reset per tick triggers chart redraw exactly once

member.name: P:PulseMonitor.ViewModels.MainViewModel.AiDiagnostics
member.summary: Exposed so the UI can bind the AI tab to this VM.

member.name: M:PulseMonitor.ViewModels.MainViewModel.OnCurrentTabIndexChanged(System.Int32)
member.summary: Executes the logic for when just changed.
member.param: The new property value that was set.
member.remarks: This method is invoked right after the value of is changed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.ColorAnimationExtensions_Button.TextColorTo(Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Button,Microsoft.Maui.Graphics.Color,System.UInt32,System.UInt32,Microsoft.Maui.Easing,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Animates the TextColor of an to the given color
member.param: The target color to animate the to
The time, in milliseconds, between the frames of the animation
The duration, in milliseconds, of the animation
The easing function to be used in the animation
member.returns: Value indicating if the animation completed successfully or not
